title: Ulex salzmannii
intro: Ulex salzmannii, also known as the Salzmann's gorse, is a spiny, evergreen shrub native to the western Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ulex salzmannii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ulex salzmannii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support overall health and well-being, particularly in traditional folk medicine.
taxonomy: Ulex salzmannii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea family. It belongs to the genus Ulex, which is a group of spiny shrubs commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The genus Ulex is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae, which consists of plants with flowers that are similar to those of legumes. Ulex salzmannii is a member of the tribe Genisteae, a group of legumes that are characterized by their spiny stems and leaves. Within the tribe Genisteae, Ulex salzmannii is a unique species with distinctive characteristics.

side_effects: Ulex salzmannii, also known as the Spanish gorse or Salzmann's furze, is a plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. While Ulex salzmannii is not typically known to cause severe side effects, contact with its sharp thorns can lead to skin irritation and minor injuries. Ingesting any part of the plant may cause gastrointestinal upset and potential allergic reactions. The sap of Ulex salzmannii may also cause skin irritation in some individuals, although this is rare. It is essential to exercise caution when handling the plant due to its prickly nature.
